title: FOLD-FISH step-by-step protocol
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f7hdq","text":"DNA fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) methods based on the use of oligonucleotide (oligo) probes have emerged as powerful tools to study genome architecture in single cells. However, their application to formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) tissues—the clinical gold standard for histopathology—remains challenging. Here, we develop and optimize FFPE OLigo-based DNA FISH (FOLD-FISH), a robust protocol for oligo-based DNA FISH on FFPE tissue samples. We demonstrate FOLD-FISH technical performance across diverse human tumor samples and genomic targets and show that it enables robust quantification of selected DNA loci copy number, in both surgical resections and core biopsies, even at low magnification (20× air objectives). To facilitate the adoption of FOLD-FISH in cancer research and diagnostics, we designed probes targeting 1,026 genes included in the OncoKB and COSMIC databases, which can be readily used to detect genes commonly assayed in diagnostics. Finally, we leveraged FOLD-FISH chromosome-spotting probes to visualize and quantitatively assess chromosome 17 territories in two breast cancer tissues, setting the stage for an atlas of chromosomal territories in tumor samples. FOLD-FISH is a versatile protocol for oligo-based DNA FISH in FFPE samples, with broad applications in pathological diagnostics, intratumor heterogeneity characterization, and 3D genome profiling in clinical samples.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":357,"length":1},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":362,"length":2},{"style":"UNDERLINE","offset":374,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: 85% (v/v) Ethanol solution Mix 170 mL of absolute Ethanol into 30 mL of nuclease-free water.

Pre-hybridization solution D (PHD): 1x PBS, 100 millimolar (mM) Tris-Cl ( pH 9 ) Mix 15 mL of 10x PBS solution and 15 mL of 1 Molarity (M) Tris-Cl ( pH 9 ) solution into 120 mL of nuclease-free water.
1x PBS solution Mix 100 mL of 10x PBS solution into 900 mL of nuclease-free water.
20% (v/v) Triton X-100 solution Mix 20 mL of Triton X-100 solution into 80 mL of nuclease-free water.

Pre-hybridization solution C (PHC): 100 millimolar (mM) Tris-Cl pH 9 , 0.2% SDS, 1 millimolar (mM) EDTA, 10 millimolar (mM) DTT Mix 15 mL of 1 Molarity (M) Tris-HCl ( pH 9 ) solution, 3 mL of 10% SDS solution and 0.3 mL of 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA solution into 130.2 mL of nuclease-free water. Add 1.5 mL of 1 Molarity (M) DTT solution right before use.

Pre-hybridization solution B (PHB): 0.01 Molarity (M) Sodium citrate buffer, 0.05% Tween 20 Mix 1.5 mL of 1 Molarity (M) sodium citrate buffer and 0.375 mL of 20% Tween 20 solution into 148.125 mL of nuclease-free water.
Pre-hybridization solution A (PHA): 0.1x SSC, 20% glycerol pH 7 , pH 5 . Mix 0.75 mL of 20x SCC solution into 119.25 mL of nuclease-free water. Then slowly add 30 mL of 100% glycerol.
20% (v/v) Tween 20 solution Mix 20 mL of Tween 20 solution into 80 mL of nuclease-free water.
50% (v/v) Ethanol solution Mix 100 mL of absolute Ethanol into 100 mL of nuclease-free water.
70% (v/v) Ethanol solution Mix 140 mL of absolute Ethanol into 70 mL of nuclease-free water.

FA50 solution: 2x SSC, 50 millimolar (mM) sodium phosphate buffer ( pH 7.6 ), 50% formamide Mix 15 mL of 20x SSC solution and 15 mL of 0.5 Molarity (M) sodium phosphate buffer ( pH 7.6 ) into 45 mL of nuclease-free water. Add 75 mL of 100% formamide solution under a chemical hood.
Collagenase III stock solution ( 10 mg/ml ) Dilute 100 mg of collagenase III into 10 mL of nuclease-free water.
Wash solution A (WSA): 0.1x SSC Mix 2.5 mL of 20x SSC solution into 497.5 mL of nuclease-free water.
Pepsin stock solution ( 2 mg/ml ) Dissolved 2 mg of pepsin into 1 mL of nuclease-free water.
0.1 Molarity (M) HCl solution Mix 50 mL of 10x PBS solution and 4.1 mL of 38% HCL into 445.9 mL of nuclease-free water.
1x PBS, 0.5% Triton X-100 solution Mix 100 mL of 10x PBS solution and 25 mL of 20% Triton X-100 solution into 875 mL of nuclease-free water.
1x PBS, 0.05% Triton X-100 solution Mix 100 mL of 10x PBS solution and 2.5 mL of 20% Triton X-100 solution into 897.5 mL of nuclease-free water.
Wash solution B (WSB): 2x SSC, 0.2% Tween 20 Mix 50 mL of 20x SSC solution and 5 mL of 20% Tween 20 solution into 445 mL of nuclease-free water.

Primary hybridization solution 2 (PHS-2): 5.55x Denhardt’s solution, 2.22x SSC, 55.5 millimolar (mM) sodium phosphate buffer, 1.11 millimolar (mM) EDTA, 111 μg/mL UltraPure Salmon Sperm DNA Solution, 11.1% (w/v) dextran sulfate, 55.5% formamide Mix the following reagents in the indicated order: 1.11 mL of 50x Denhardt’s solution 1.11 mL of 20x SSC solution 1.11 mL of 0.5 Molarity (M) sodium phosphate buffer 22.2 µL of 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA solution 111 µL of 10 mg/ml UltraPure Salmon Sperm DNA Solution 1.1 g of dextran sulfate 5.5 mL of 100% formamide solution Adjust the pH to pH 8.0 – pH 8.5 and bring the volume up to 10 mL with nuclease-free water.

Primary hybridization solution 1 (PHS-1): 5x Denhardt’s solution, 2x SSC, 50 millimolar (mM) sodium phosphate buffer, 1 millimolar (mM) EDTA, 100 μg/mL UltraPure Salmon Sperm DNA Solution, 50% formamide Mix the following reagents in the indicated order: 1 mL of 50x Denhardt’s solution 1 mL of 20x SSC solution 1 mL of the 0.5 Molarity (M) sodium phosphate buffer 20 µL of 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA solution 100 µL of 10 mg/ml UltraPure Salmon Sperm DNA Solution 5 mL of 100% formamide solution Adjust the pH to pH 8.0 – pH 8.5 and bring the volume up to 10 mL with nuclease-free water.
PFA quenching solution: 1x PBS, 125 millimolar (mM) glycine Mix 15 mL of 10x PBS solution and 15 mL of 1.25 Molarity (M) glycine solution into 120 mL of nuclease-free water.
1.25 Molarity (M) glycine solution Dissolved 93 g , 73 g of glycine into 1 L of nuclease-free water and sterilize the solution with 0.22 μm pore size membrane filter.
Post-fixation solution (PFS): 1x PBS, 1% paraformaldehyde Mix 15 mL of 10x PBS into 125.625 mL of nuclease-free water, then add 9.375 mL of 16% PFA solution under a chemical hood.
Methanol/acetic acid (MAA) solution Mix 112.5 mL of Methanol and 37.5 mL of Acetic Acid.

Detection hybridization solution (DHS): 2x SSC, 1 mg/ml E.coli tRNA, 0.02% BSA, 25% formamide and 10% (w/v) dextran sulfate Mix the following reagents in the indicated order: 1 mL of 20x SSC solution 500 µL of 20 mg/ml E.coli tRNA 40 µL of 50 mg/ml UltraPure BSA 1 g of dextran sulfate 2.5 mL of 100% formamide solution Adjust the pH to pH 8.0 – pH 8.5 and bring the volume up to 10 mL with nuclease-free water.
Wash solution E (WSE): 2x SSC, 25% formamide Mix 15 mL of 20x SSC solution into 97.5 mL of nuclease-free water, then add 37.5 mL of 100% FA under a chemical hood.
Wash solution D (WSD): 4x SSC, 0.2% Tween 20 Mix 100 mL of 20x SSC solution and 5 mL of 20% Tween 20 solution into 395 mL of nuclease-free water.
Wash solution C (WSC): 0.2x SSC, 0.2% Tween 20 Mix 5 mL of 20x SSC solution and 5 mL of 20% Tween 20 solution into 490 mL of nuclease-free water.

Unless otherwise specified, solutions are prepared and used at room temperature. Use maximum 6 slides per staining jar. CRITICAL: We recommend performing all the following steps in a fume hood, as xylene is classified as a hazardous chemical that causes health hazards. Except for step 30, all the other steps are performed using tissue staining jars (for example, Electron Microscopy Sciences, Cat. No. 71385-Y).
Bake the FFPE slides at 60 °C for 01:00:00 in an incubator (for example, Boekel Scientific 241000).

Incubate the slides in 100% ethanol solution for 00:03:00 at Room temperature . CRITICAL: Before transferring the slides into the 100% ethanol solution, gently absorb any remaining xylene solution by gently placing the shortest side of each slide (opposite to the white, slide-handling side) against a piece of tissue paper. Do not touch the tissue section with the tissue.
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 100% ethanol solution.
Repeat steps 31–32, by transferring the slides into a new jar pre-filled with fresh xylene solution.
Incubate the slides in xylene solution for 00:03:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with xylene solution.
Transfer the slides into a new jar pre-filled with fresh 100% ethanol solution.
Incubate the slides in 100% ethanol solution for 00:03:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 85% ethanol solution.
Incubate the slides in 85% ethanol solution for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 70% ethanol solution.
Incubate the slides in 70% ethanol solution for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 50% ethanol solution.
Incubate the slides in 50% ethanol solution for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with nuclease-free water.
Incubate the slides in nuclease-free water for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
CRITICAL: Unless otherwise specified, the following steps are performed in tissue staining jars. Do not use more than 6 slides per jar.
Fill in three tissue staining jars with PHA, PHB, and PHC solution, respectively, and place them in a water bath at 90 °C (for PHA) and 80 °C (for PHB and PHC).
Transfer the slides from step 45 into the jar containing the PHA solution.
Incubate the slides in PHA solution for 00:10:00 at 90 °C .
Transfer the slides into the jar containing the PHB solution.
Incubate the slides in PHB solution for 00:45:00 at 80 °C .
Transfer the slides into the jar containing the PHC solution.
Incubate the slides in PHC solution for 00:20:00 at 80 °C .
Transfer the jar containing the slides in PHC solution to Room temperature .
Let the PHC solution cool down over a period of 00:30:00 until it reaches Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 1x PBS solution.
Quickly wash the slides in 1x PBS solution at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 1x PBS, 0.05% Triton X-100 solution.
Place the jar with the slides in 1x PBS, 0.05% Triton X-100 solution on a shaking platform, gently shaking.
Incubate the slides in 1x PBS, 0.05% Triton X-100 solution for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Repeat steps 58–60 twice, by transferring the slides into a new jar containing fresh 1x PBS, 0.05% Triton X-100 solution.
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 1x PBS, 0.5% Triton X-100 solution.
Incubate the slides in 1x PBS, 0.5% Triton X-100 solution for 00:20:00 at Room temperature . CRITICAL: Do not shake the jar in this step.
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 1x PBS, 0.05% Triton X-100 solution.
Place the jar with the slides in 1x PBS, 0.05% Triton X-100 solution on a shaking platform, gently shaking.
Repeat steps 64–65 twice, each time transferring the slides into a new jar containing fresh 1x PBS, 0.5% Triton X-100 solution.
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 0.1 Molarity (M) HCl solution.
Quickly wash the slides in 0.1 Molarity (M) HCl solution at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a new jar pre-filled with fresh 0.1 Molarity (M) HCl solution.

Incubate the slides in 0.1 Molarity (M) HCl solution up to 00:05:00 at Room temperature . CRITICAL: The total incubation time in HCl solution starting from step 68 until step 70 should not exceed 5 min in total.
Transfer the slides into a new jar pre-filled with 1x PBS, 0.05% Triton X-100 solution.
Quickly wash the slides in 1x PBS, 0.05% Triton X-100 solution at Room temperature .
Repeat steps 71–72 twice, each time transferring the slides into a new jar containing fresh 1x PBS, 0.05% Triton X-100 solution.
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 1x PBS solution.
Incubate the slides in 1x PBS solution for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .

Transfer the slides from the jar containing 1x PBS solution onto the tray provided with the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven. CRITICAL: Avoid drying the slides by covering them with a sufficient volume of 1x PBS solution using a 3 mL Pasteur pipette.
Using a hydrophobic pen, mark the boundary of each tissue section and let the mark completely dry at Room temperature .

Prepare the pepsin working solution by diluting the pepsin stock solution in 0.01 Molarity (M) HCl solution at a final concentration of 0.05 mg/ml . Prepare the following mix by adjusting the volumes depending on the number of slides: A B 1 Pepsin working solution Volume for 1 slide 2 Pepsin stock solution (2 mg/ml) 5 μL 3 0.1 M HCL 20 μL 4 Nuclease-free water 175 μL 5 Total volume 200 μL
Pre-warm the pepsin working solution at 37 °C for 00:10:00 .
Immediately dispense 200 µL of pre-warmed pepsin working solution onto each tissue section. CRITICAL: Make sure to completely cover the tissue sections.

Incubate the tissue sections covered by pepsin working solution for 00:10:00 at 37 °C in the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven. CRITICAL: The incubation time should be optimized when working with a new tissue type.
Take the slides out of the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven and tap each slide on a piece of absorbent paper to remove any remaining traces of pepsin working solution.
Immediately place the slides in a staining jar pre-filled with WSA solution.
Incubate the slides in WSA solution for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Repeat steps 83–84, by transferring the slides to a new jar containing fresh WSA solution.

Prepare the collagenase III working solution by diluting the collagenase III stock solution ( 10 mg/ml ) to a final concentration of 0.5 mg/ml with nuclease-free water. Prepare the following mix by adjusting the volumes depending on the number of slides: %s %s
Transfer the slides from the jar containing the WSA solution (step 85) onto the tray provided with the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven..
Dispense 200 µL of collagenase III working solution onto each tissue section. CRITICAL: Use enough solution so that each tissue section is completely covered by liquid.
Incubate the tissue sections covered by collagenase III working solution for 00:20:00 at 37 °C in the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven.
Take the slides out of the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven and tap each slide on a piece of absorbent paper to remove any remaining traces of collagenase III working solution.
Immediately place the slides in a staining jar pre-filled with WSA solution.
Incubate the slides in WSA solution for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Repeat steps 91–92, by transferring the slides to a new jar containing fresh WSA solution.
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with FA50 solution.
Quickly wash the slides in FA50 solution at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a new jar pre-filled with fresh FA50 solution and cover the jar with its glass lid.

Incubate the slides in FA50 solution with Overnight at Room temperature protected from light, by wrapping a piece of aluminum foil around the staining jar and placing it in a drawer or cabinet. CRITICAL: We recommend keeping the jar containing the FA50 solution in a fume hood as formamide is classified as hazardous chemical causing carcinogenicity, reproductive toxicity and target organ toxicity following repeated exposure.
Remove the slides from the FA50 solution (step 97) and transfer them into a jar pre-filled with PHD solution.
Repeat steps 100–101, by transferring the slides to a new jar containing fresh WSA solution.
Incubate the slides in WSA solution for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with fresh WSA solution.
Incubate the slides in PHD solution for 00:10:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with freshly prepared PFS solution.
Repeat steps 105–106, by transferring the slides to a new jar containing fresh WSA solution.
Incubate the slides in WSA solution for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with fresh WSA solution.
Incubate the slides in MAA solution for 00:30:00 at -20 °C .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with MAA solution pre-chilled at -20 °C .
Incubate the slides in 1x PBS solution for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 1x PBS solution.
Incubate the slides in PFA quenching solution for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with PFA quenching solution.
Incubate the slides in PFS solution for 00:05:00 at Room temperature .
Repeat steps 112–113, by transferring the slides to a new jar containing fresh 1x PBS solution.
Fill in three tissue staining jars with 70%, 85%, and 100% Ethanol solution, respectively.
Repeat steps 120–121, by transferring the slides to a new jar containing fresh 100% Ethanol solution.
Incubate the slides in 100% Ethanol solution for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into the jar pre-filled with 100% Ethanol solution
Incubate the slides in 85% Ethanol solution for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into the jar pre-filled with 85% Ethanol solution
Incubate the slides in 70% Ethanol solution for 00:02:00 at Room temperature .
Remove the slides from the 1x PBS solution (step 114) and transfer them into the jar pre-filled with 70% Ethanol solution.
Transfer the slides onto a slide rack and place the rack onto the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven tray without closing the lid.
Place the tray inside the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven and dry the slides for 00:30:00 at 60 °C .
Take the slides out from the oven and cover each tissue section with PHS-1 solution, while keeping the slides onto the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven tray.
Perform DNA denaturation for 00:08:00 at 88 °C . CRITICAL: The denaturation time should be optimized when working with a new tissue type.
Add 20 µL of Hybridization Mix onto each tissue section.

Meanwhile, prepare the Hybridization Mix by mixing the following reagents and adjusting the volumes based on the number of tissue sections: %s %s CRITICAL: The concentration of the primary probe should be optimized when working with a new tissue type.
Incubate the slides for 00:45:00 at 37 °C inside the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven.
Transfer the slides back into the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven.
Transfer the slides to the ThermoBrite denaturization system.

Cover the section with a glass coverslip, then seal the coverslip’s margins with Fixogum. CRITICAL: Be careful while pipetting the Hybridization Mix onto the tissue section and while lowering the coverslip onto the solution, to avoid bubble formation.
Incubate the slides Overnight at 37 °C inside the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven.
Quickly take the slides out of the ThermoBrite machine and transfer them onto the tray.
While denaturation is ongoing, prepare a humidity chamber by placing several stripes of tissue paper pre-wet in water all around the edges of the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven tray.
Fill in two staining jars with WSC solution and place them in a water bath pre-warmed at 65 °C .
Transfer the slides into a new jar pre-filled with WSD solution.
Repeat steps 141–142, by transferring the slides to the second jar containing WSC solution pre-warmed at 65 °C .
Incubate the slides in WSC solution for 00:07:00 at 65 °C .
Transfer the slides into the jar pre-filled with WSC solution pre-warmed at 65 °C .
Repeat steps 138-139, by transferring the slides to a new jar containing fresh WSB solution.
Incubate the slides in WSB solution for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a staining jar pre-filled with fresh WSB solution.
Remove the slides from the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven tray (Step 135) and gently remove the Fixogum and the coverslips by placing each slide in a 10 cm dish pre-filled with WSB solution.
Transfer the slides into a humidity chamber prepared using the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven tray (see step 133).
Quickly wash the slides in WSE solution.
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with WSE solution.
Quickly wash the slides in 2x SSC solution.
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 2x SSC solution.
Incubate the slides in WSD solution for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Add 100 µL of Detection Hybridization Mix onto each tissue section.
%s %s CRITICAL: The concentration of the signal-amplification and detection oligos should be optimized when working with a new tissue type.
While keeping the slides in WSE solution, prepare the Detection Hybridization Mix by mixing the following reagents and adjusting the volumes based on the number of tissue sections:
Repeat steps 148–149, by transferring the slides into a new jar pre-filled with fresh WSE solution.

Cover the section with a small piece of Parafilm to prevent evaporation. CRITICAL: Be careful while pipetting the Hybridization Detection Mix onto the tissue section and while lowering the piece of Parafilm onto the solution, to avoid bubble formation.
Incubate the slides Overnight at 30 °C inside the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven.
CRITICAL: Throughout the following steps, minimize the exposure of the slides to light.
Fill in two staining jars with WSC solution and place them in a water bath pre-warmed at 45 °C .
Remove the slides from the Boekel Inslide Out Hybridization Oven tray (Step 156) and transfer the slides into a staining jar pre-filled with fresh WSB solution.
Gently remove the pieces of Parafilm that detached from the slides.
Incubate the slides in WSB solution for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides to a new jar containing fresh WSB solution.
Quickly wash the slides in WSB solution.
Transfer the slides into the jar pre-filled with WSC solution pre-warmed at 45 °C .
Incubate the slides in WSC solution for 00:07:00 at 45 °C .
Fill in a staining jar with the DNA Staining Mix prepared in the previous step. If more than 6 slides need to be processed, fill in more jars.
While keeping the slides in 2x SSC solution, prepare the DNA Staining Mix by mixing the following reagents and adjusting the volumes based on the number of tissue sections: %s %s
Transfer the slides into a jar pre-filled with 2x SSC solution.
Incubate the slides in WSD solution for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides into a new jar pre-filled with WSD solution.
Repeat steps 164–165, by transferring the slides to the second jar containing WSC solution pre-warmed at 45 °C .
Transfer the slides into a new jar pre-filled with fresh 1X PBS solution.
Incubate the slides in DNA Staining Mix for 00:20:00 at Room temperature .
Transfer the slides from 2x SSC solution (step 169) into the jar containing the DNA Staining Mix.
Quickly wash the slides in 1X PBS solution.
Repeat steps 174–175 twice, each time transferring the slides into a new jar pre-filled with fresh 1X PBS solution.
Remove one slide from the 1X PBS solution and tap it on a piece of absorbent paper to remove any remaining traces of 1X PBS solution.
Add 10 µL of ProLong Diamond Antifade Mountant onto the tissue section (10 mm x 10 mm).

Cover the section with a glass coverslip. CRITICAL: Be careful while pipetting the ProLong Diamond Antifade Mountant onto the tissue section and while lowering the coverslip onto the solution, to avoid bubble formation.
Incubate the slides Overnight at Room temperature , protected from light. If the slides are not imaged on the next day, they can be stored for few months at -20 °C .
